Mr.. and Mrs. Newland will make
their home in this city at $50 Ter
race avenue.


MOUNT CARLTON CLUB is


ENTERTAINED.


The members of the Mount Carl
ton club were guests yesterday of
Mrs. e. A. Rudder at her home,
E35 Mallon avenue. A social hour
was enjoyed and luncheon was
served to the IA ladies who were
present.


PICNIC AT MANiTO.
About 100 young people, belong
ing to the Union Park Evangelical
church, joined in the picnic celebra-
ton which was given today at
Mantle park. A short program was
rendered, after which the hours
were spent with games and in a
general merrymaking. A basket
luncheon was served.


Z E Z


PICNIC ON TUESDAY.
The employees of the Singer
Sewing Machine Co. held a picnic at
Natatorium park Tuesday evening.
Following the supper, which was
served at $50 O'Clock, they visited
the various attractions and spent a
delightful evening.


4 & =


L. o. r. N. HAVE OUTING.
The members of hive No. 10,
Ladies of the Maccabees of the
World, are picnicking today at Nat
atOrium park. Their husbands and
friends have ben invited to join
them this evening.


4 q 4
SUFFRAGE MEETING TONIGHT
From present indications large
crowd will attend the suffrage meet
ing to be held this evening at the
Dean Avenue Christian church.
Rev Bruce Brown and Mrs. Min
ME j. Reynolds will be the speakers
of the evening.


=


ICE CREAM SOCIAL.
An ice cream social will be give
this evening at Glass park by the
members of the Kenwood Christian
church.
